title: Rajasthani Gatte Ki Sabzi

subs.name: no direct substitute — besan is the defining ingredient of gatte
2 tbsp lemon juice + same volume of water if curd is unavailable; the gravy will be thinner
equal quantity cumin seeds; flavour shifts slightly but gatte still work
2 tbsp lemon juice + same volume of water if curd is unavailable; the gravy will be thinner
no direct substitute — besan is the defining ingredient of gatte

steps.text: Make the gatte dough: combine 1.5 cups besan, 3 tbsp sour curd, ajwain, 0.5 tsp red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp oil and salt. Mix and knead into a stiff, non-sticky dough — stiffer than roti dough. Divide into 4 portions and roll each into a smooth cylinder about 1.5 cm in diameter.

Bring a large pot of well-salted water to a boil. Drop in the gatte cylinders and cook on a full boil for 12–14 minutes until firm to the touch and the surface looks slightly textured rather than smooth. A cut cylinder should show no raw besan inside. Drain, reserving 1 cup of the cooking water.

Once cool enough to handle, slice the cooked gatte into 1.5 cm roundels. If you like a bit of extra texture, shallow-fry the sliced gatte in a little oil over medium heat until lightly golden on each side, then set aside.

Whisk together 1 cup sour curd, 1 tbsp besan and the remaining 0.25 tsp turmeric in a bowl until completely smooth. Heat 3 tbsp oil in a kadai on medium-high. Add cumin and dried red chillies; let them sizzle for 15 seconds. Add asafoetida and onions; sauté until deep golden, about 7 minutes.

Add ginger-garlic paste and fry for a minute. Add tomatoes, remaining red chilli powder, coriander powder and salt; cook until tomatoes are completely broken down and oil surfaces — about 6 minutes. Lower the flame and pour in the curd-besan mixture, stirring constantly to prevent curdling.

Add 0.75 cup of the reserved gatte-cooking water and bring the gravy to a gentle simmer. Add the gatte roundels and cook together for 5 minutes so the gatte absorb the curry. Stir in garam masala, taste and adjust salt. Finish with coriander leaves and serve hot with steamed rice or bajra roti.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: curd → 2 tbsp lemon juice + same volume of water if curd is unavailable; the gravy will be thinner; ajwain → equal quantity cumin seeds; flavour shifts slightly but gatte still work; besan → no direct substitute — besan is the defining ingredient of gatte
